title: Houses for Rent in Eastlake, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Eastlake, OH (Lake County)
metaDescription: Searching for houses for rent in Eastlake, Ohio? Get honest Lake County location facts, Cleveland-area rent benchmarks, and voucher notes, then contact us.
answerBox: Eastlake is a Lake County city on Lake Erie, in the same northeastern lakefront corridor as Willowick and Wickliffe, beyond Cleveland's northeast side. Rent Finder Cleveland's managed homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and northeast side neighborhoods, not Lake County, so the fastest path to a real answer is telling our team what you need and what's nearby.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Eastlake, Ohio
body: Eastlake sits in Lake County on the Lake Erie shoreline, grouped with Willowick and Wickliffe as part of the same northeastern lakefront communities, further out than Cleveland's own northeast-side neighborhoods. It's a separate county jurisdiction from Cleveland and its Cuyahoga County suburbs, which matters for both rent conventions and voucher administration, covered below.

Rent Finder Cleveland's rental portfolio is concentrated in Cleveland's east and northeast side neighborhoods, and we don't currently manage listed homes specifically in Eastlake or elsewhere in Cuyahoga County's suburbs. If Eastlake is where you need to be, [reach out and describe your situation](/p/book-showing) — bedroom count, budget, timeline, and whether you carry a housing voucher — and we'll tell you honestly what's realistic, including options in the nearest Cleveland neighborhoods we do serve.

sections.h2: Cleveland-area rent benchmarks for Eastlake renters
body: We don't operate in Eastlake, so there's no Eastlake-specific figure to quote, but the wider Cleveland market gives a useful reference point for anyone comparing Lake County options against the city. Zumper's rent research, dated July 4, 2026, puts the Cleveland-area median asking rent at $1,250 a month, a 4.2% increase year-over-year. RentCafe's (Yardi) report, dated July 2, 2026, puts the average rent across professionally managed Cleveland-area apartments at $1,564 a month for roughly 787 square feet.

The gap between those two figures comes down to methodology: RentCafe's average leans toward larger, newer managed buildings, while Zumper's median reflects the broader mix of listings, including older and smaller units. Either way, both sources place Cleveland-area rent well under the $1,950 national median Zumper reports — about 36% lower.

sections.h2: Housing vouchers and Section 8 near Eastlake
body: Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for Cuyahoga County are administered by the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority ([CMHA](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php)). Eastlake is in Lake County, outside CMHA's jurisdiction, so a voucher holder looking to rent there should ask their caseworker which public housing authority actually covers Lake County addresses and what the port-in process looks like before assuming CMHA rules apply.

It's also worth knowing that Ohio has no statewide law requiring a landlord to accept a housing voucher as payment. As of our July 2026 research, source-of-income protections for voucher holders exist only in a small set of Cuyahoga County suburbs — Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale — and none of our sources show a comparable ordinance in Eastlake or elsewhere in Lake County. Confirm current local rules directly before counting on a specific address.

Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready, without exception — that guarantee just doesn't currently extend to a Lake County address. See our [Section 8 housing guide for Cleveland](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the process works where we do operate.

The closest homes in our portfolio sit in Cleveland's North Collinwood area, along the same Lake Erie shoreline corridor within ZIP code 44110, before that shoreline crosses into Lake County. Every home we manage there accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. [Check North Collinwood availability](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-north-collinwood-cleveland/) if a Cleveland lakefront address works for you.

If you want to keep comparing Lake County options directly, our [Euclid rental guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-euclid-ohio/) covers a neighboring lakefront community closer to Cleveland — again, for reference rather than confirmed availability, since we manage no listed homes there today.

sections.h2: How to get a real answer on availability
body: A static number on this page would go stale fast, so the honest approach is to ask directly. [Contact our leasing team](/p/book-showing) with your bedroom count, budget, and voucher status, and we'll tell you plainly whether we have anything close to Eastlake right now or whether a nearby Cleveland neighborhood is the better fit. Our [Cleveland rentals hub](/cleveland-rentals/) also links out to every suburb and neighborhood guide we publish, including areas outside our current footprint like Eastlake, so you can keep comparing before you decide.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses for rent in Eastlake, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our portfolio is concentrated in Cleveland's east and northeast side neighborhoods, and we have no listed homes specifically in Eastlake, which is in Lake County. Contact us with your needs and we'll point you to the closest real options, including nearby Cleveland lakefront neighborhoods.

faq.q: What does a house rent for in the Cleveland area right now?
faq.a: Per Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, the Cleveland-area median asking rent is $1,250 a month. Per RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, the average across professionally managed apartments is $1,564 a month. Neither number is specific to Eastlake, since we don't track that market directly.

faq.q: Does CMHA administer Section 8 vouchers in Eastlake?
faq.a: No. CMHA is the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority and covers Cuyahoga County, not Lake County, where Eastlake is located. Voucher holders should ask their caseworker which housing authority has jurisdiction over Lake County and how porting a voucher there works.

faq.q: Where is the closest Section 8-friendly rental home to Eastlake?
faq.a: Our nearest managed homes are in Cleveland's North Collinwood neighborhood, ZIP code 44110, along the Lake Erie shoreline. Every home we manage there accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our footprint currently stops before it reaches Eastlake itself.

faq.q: Are landlords in Eastlake required to accept a housing voucher?
faq.a: Ohio has no statewide law requiring it, and none of our sources show a local ordinance in Eastlake protecting voucher holders the way a handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs do. Confirm current rules with the housing authority covering Lake County before relying on a specific unit accepting a voucher.
